Feature_Name: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup Procedure

3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN; Architecture description

Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (L1/L2 Triggered Mobility).

The purpose of the UE Context Setup procedure is to establish the UE Context including, among others, SRB, DRB, BH RLC channel, Uu Relay RLC channel, PC5 Relay RLC channel, and SL DRB configuration in the candidate gNB-DU for the LTM handover.
Feature_Objectives: To establish the UE Context in the candidate gNB-DU for Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
To configure LTM-specific resources and information in the candidate gNB-DU.
To enable early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation) for the LTM candidate cell.
To provide generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted LTM candidate cell to the gNB-CU.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 Measurement Reports from the UE.

2. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

3. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU initiates the procedure by sending a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU.

2. The UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message includes the LTM Information Setup IE (containing LTM Indicator IE, Reference Configuration IE, CSI Resource Configuration IE), LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE, Early Sync Information Request IE, and SpCell ID IE.
3. If the LTM Indicator IE in the LTM Information Setup IE is set to "true", the gNB-DU shall consider the request concerns LTM for the included SpCell ID IE.

4. If the Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE in the Reference Configuration IE is set to "true", the gNB-DU shall provide the lower layer configuration in the Reference Configuration Information IE in the LTM Configuration IE in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
5. If the Reference Configuration Information IE is contained in the Reference Configuration IE, the gNB-DU shall take it into account for generating the LTM lower layer configuration.

6. If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE, the gNB-DU shall use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration(s) in the CellGroupConfig IE for the requested LTM candidate cell.
7. If the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE is included, the gNB-DU shall consider it as mapping information for the LTM candidate cell(s).

8. If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included, the gNB-DU shall include the Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
9. If the gNB-DU succeeds to establish the UE context and LTM configuration, it replies to the gNB-CU with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.

10. The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message includes the Requested Target Cell ID IE, Early Sync Information IE (containing Early UL Sync Configuration IE and Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE), and LTM Configuration IE (containing Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE and Reference Configuration Information IE).
11. If the LTM Configuration IE is included, the gNB-CU shall consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell in the candidate gNB-DU.

12. If the Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE in the LTM Configuration IE is set to "complete", the gNB-CU shall consider that the LTM candidate configuration is a complete candidate configuration.

13. If the gNB-DU is not able to establish an F1 UE context or cannot establish one bearer, it shall reply with the UE CONTEXT SETUP FAILURE message, including the received SpCell ID IE as the Requested Target Cell ID IE.

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: The UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST and UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE messages can carry RRC information elements (IEs) transparently or for specific processing. The CU to DU RRC Information IE in the request can include RRC messages like RRCReconfiguration or configuration information like CellGroupConfig, HandoverPreparationInformation, MeasurementTimingConfiguration, and MeasConfig. The DU to CU RRC Information IE in the response can include RRC information such as ServingCellMO-encoded-in-CGC List, Configured BWP List, MeasGapConfig, ServCellInfoList, Full Configuration, SDT-MAC-PHY-CG-Config, InterFrequencyConfig-NoGap, ul-GapFR2-Config, TwoPHRModeMCG, TwoPHRModeSCG, ncd-SSB-RedCapInitialBWP-SDT, and MUSIM-GapConfig.
